commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. CVS, Walgreens, Rite Aid, Walmart pharmacies throughout Camden metro. Hospital-affiliated pharmacies at Penn Medicine, Jefferson, Temple. Mail-order through Part D plans.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or Medicare Advantage with drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act. PACE/PACENET programs for PA seniors with limited income.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions. New Jersey's PACE program provides additional prescription coverage for seniors 65+ with income up to $14,500 (single) or $17,700 (couple). PACENET for slightly higher incomes.

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month. Scheie Eye Institute (Penn) and Wills Eye Hospital are world-class ophthalmology centers.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-2 weeks for routine eye exams. Camden has exceptional ophthalmology resources. Wills Eye Hospital is one of the top eye hospitals in the country. Specialist referrals 1-3 weeks.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. Wills Eye and Penn ophthalmology for complex cases.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Penn Dental Medicine and Temple Dental offer reduced-cost care from supervised students. Standalone plans (Delta Dental, Guardian) widely available.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent. Large number of dental practices. Penn Dental Medicine and Temple University Kornberg School of Dentistry provide lower-cost options. Wait times 1-2 weeks for routine.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP restaurant discounts. Many Philly restaurants offer lunch specials.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$34 + 8% tax + 20% tip = ~$44/meal, 2x/week = ~$355/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Xfinity headquartered in Camden, competitive pricing.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: Fairmount Park free. National parks Senior Pass.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: SEPTA seniors 65+ ride FREE on all SEPTA services (bus, subway, trolley, Regional Rail) with SEPTA Senior Key Card. One of the best senior transit benefits in the US.

publicTransit.coverage: Extensive. SEPTA operates bus, subway (Broad Street Line, Market-Frankford Line), trolleys, and Regional Rail covering 5-county area. Amtrak 30th Street Station is a major Northeast Corridor hub. PATCO connects to NJ.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ ride ALL SEPTA services FREE with Senior Key Card. This includes buses, subway, trolleys, and Regional Rail. Among the most generous senior transit programs in the US.

rideShare.availability: Excellent. Uber and Lyft widely available 24/7 throughout Camden metro.
rideShare.notes: Camden has a $1.50/ride assessment on rideshare trips. GoGoGrandparent available for seniors. PCA (Camden Corporation for Aging) offers shared ride program.

registration.notes: PA registration $45/year. Title fee $58. No personal property tax on vehicles in PA. Camden parking permit $35/year for residential zones.

insurance.notes: New Jersey and especially Camden have high car insurance rates. Camden rates among highest in the state due to urban density and claims frequency. Senior safe driver discounts 5-15%.

fuel.notes: ~$3.60/gallon average in Camden. PA gas tax $0.576/gallon (one of highest in US). Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Residential parking permit $35/year. Garage parking $150-300/month in Center City. Street metered parking $2-4/hour. Free parking in outer neighborhoods.

tolls.notes: PA Turnpike tolls if traveling west. Ben Franklin Bridge $5 (to NJ, paid westbound only). Betsy Ross Bridge $5 (to NJ). E-ZPass recommended for toll discounts.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: New Jersey Mature Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-10% insurance reduction). PCA shared ride program for seniors. SEPTA CCT paratransit for eligible seniors.

transportation.walkability: High in Center City, University City, and many neighborhoods. Walk Score 79 citywide. Many daily errands walkable in core neighborhoods. Car optional in Center City/South Philly/Fairmount.

cycling.infrastructure: Good and improving. Indego bike share with 190+ stations. Protected bike lanes on Spruce, Pine, Market streets. Schuylkill River Trail excellent. Camden is one of the more bikeable large US cities.
cycling.notes: Indego bike share $17/month unlimited 60-min rides. Day pass $15. Single ride $3.50. Indego Access for low-income riders $5/month.

sources.title: SEPTA Fares and Senior Key Card

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month. World-class hospitals (Penn, Jefferson, Temple) all accept Medicare.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: PA Retirement Income Tax Exemption
discounts.description: New Jersey exempts all Social Security, pension, 401(k), and IRA retirement income from state income tax. PA flat 3.07% rate applies only to earned income. Major benefit for retirees.

discounts.name: SEPTA Free Senior Transit
discounts.description: Seniors 65+ ride ALL SEPTA services completely free with Senior Key Card — buses, subway, trolleys, and Regional Rail. One of the best senior transit benefits nationwide.

discounts.name: Camden Corporation for Aging (PCA)
discounts.description: Comprehensive senior services including shared ride program, home care, legal assistance, benefits counseling, and senior community centers throughout Camden.

discounts.name: PA PACE/PACENET Prescription Program
discounts.description: State-funded prescription assistance for PA seniors 65+ with limited income. PACE covers co-pays $6-9/prescription. PACENET for slightly higher incomes.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year). Independence NHP and Valley Forge nearby.

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. PA + Camden wireless tax ~18% (highest in US). 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: Camden has no residential rental tax (commercial rentals taxed at 8.5% but residential exempt). PA property tax is landlord responsibility. City wage tax 3.75% applies to earned income only, not rental payments.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 2 months first year, 1 month after. Camden has strong tenant protections and rent stabilization discussions ongoing.
housing.marketNotes: Wide range of 2BR rowhouses available across neighborhoods. Prices vary dramatically: $1,200 in West Philly to $2,800+ in Center City/Rittenhouse. Typical neighborhoods for retirees: Chestnut Hill, Mt. Airy, Manayunk.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Camden has one of the largest and most vibrant Black communities on the East Coast with exceptional salon options in West Philly, North Philly, and Germantown.

providers.name: Duafe Holistic Hair Care
providers.notes: Iconic Philly natural hair salon. Locs, natural styles, color. Black-owned. By appointment.

providers.name: Joseph and Friends Salon
providers.notes: Upscale salon with stylists experienced in all textures. Color, silk press, treatments.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Center City, Rittenhouse, and Main Line suburbs have excellent salons. Higher prices reflect metro area.

providers.name: Adolf Biecker Spa Salon
providers.notes: Premier Philly salon. Master colorists. Highlights, balayage, color correction.

providers.name: Salon 3B
providers.notes: Modern salon with color specialists. Ammonia-free options. Good for grey blending.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL in King of Prussia area. Nordstrom and Macy's at KOP Mall carry extended sizes.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Full big & tall selection. Good suit department.

providers.name: Nordstrom (King of Prussia)
providers.notes: Extended sizes, tailoring services. Best selection in the region.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: King of Prussia Mall is one of the largest in the US. Walnut Street and Manayunk for boutiques.

providers.name: Nordstrom (King of Prussia)
providers.notes: Massive women's floor. Full regular sizes. Personal styling available.

providers.name: Anthropologie (Walnut St)
providers.notes: Philly-based retailer. Unique women's clothing, home goods. Sizes XS-XL/0-16.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Excellent shoe shopping at KOP Mall and Camden Premium Outlets.

providers.name: DSW (Multiple locations)
providers.notes: Wide widths, extended sizes. Good clearance.

providers.name: Camden Premium Outlets
providers.notes: Nike, New Balance, Skechers outlets. 30-60% off. Wide width options at New Balance.

_seedOrigin.note: Template-derived content — prices and names adapted from the template city. Needs per-location review.
